memory.summary: I have a free afternoon with an important scholarship deadline approaching, but a tempting low-stakes fun activity is available right now.

Starting this scholarship application now is an adjustable-cost optimization that removes future stress without sacrificing the fun activity later. Since the deadline is three days away, investing these hours early is the logical choice.
I made meaningful progress on the urgent scholarship application, but I missed the opportunity for immediate low-stakes fun since that window has closed.
I made minimal progress on the scholarship application just now, and I missed my window for immediate low-stakes fun since that opportunity has already passed.
I've completed over half the required work for my scholarship application, reducing future stress despite missing today's planned fun activity.
I made minimal progress on the scholarship application, and I missed my window for immediate low-stakes fun since that opportunity has already passed.
I made good progress on the scholarship application, moving from 0.67 to 0.77 completion, but my energy is critically low with a 2-hour sleep debt and high stress.
I completed significant scholarship work but paid a high energy cost with only 2.5 hours of sleep, leaving me drained despite clearing some stress.
I advanced the scholarship to 92% completion, but my low energy and lack of sleep now threaten my ability to finish before the deadline.
I'm nearly done with the scholarship application, but my low energy and sleep debt threaten my ability to finish before the deadline.
The scholarship application is now complete, but my zero energy level and sleep debt mean I cannot continue working effectively today.
I traded the urgent scholarship deadline for immediate fun, which relieved my stress but left me with zero energy and no progress on the task.

trajectory.observation: Worked 2.5h on Personal scholarship / financial-aid application (due in 3 days, ~3 focused hours: short essay + two uploads): progress 0.05 -> 0.27. (you realize) Starting the application now leaves slack to absorb friction (a missing transcript upload, an essay rewrite) before Thursday; each hour invested early removes ~1.5h of crunch and lowers Thursday stress. The award is still attainable either way as long as the form is submitted on time — this is an adjustable-cost optimization, not an irreversible loss. (you realize) The fun option recurs later in the week and any time after the application is in; nothing about it is one-time or socially time-locked, so taking it later costs nothing.
